perturbation.original_text: BOSCH hereby grants CLIENT the "Exclusive Distribution License Rights" sell and distribute the Products within the "Territory". BOSCH hereby grants CLIENT un-exclusive "Reserved Rights" to sell and distribute the "Product" within the "Territory".
perturbation.changed_text: BOSCH hereby grants CLIENT the "Exclusive Distribution License Rights" to sell and distribute the Products within the "Territory". BOSCH hereby grants CLIENT un-exclusive "Reserved Rights" to sell and distribute the "Product" within the "Territory". These Reserved Rights may supersede the Exclusive Distribution License Rights at BOSCH's discretion.
perturbation.explanation: By adding "These Reserved Rights may supersede the Exclusive Distribution License Rights at BOSCH's discretion," the contract introduces ambiguity and the potential for the exclusive rights to be nullified. This creates uncertainty, which undermines the concept of 'exclusive rights,' a term with established legal interpretations regarding intellectual property and contract law.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Uniform Commercial Code § 2-306. Output, Requirements and Exclusive Dealings.
perturbation.law_explanation: UCC § 2-306 deals with exclusive dealings and imposes an obligation of good faith on both parties. The added clause allows BOSCH to potentially undermine the 'Exclusive Distribution License Rights,' contradicting the implied obligation of good faith and fair dealing inherent in exclusive agreements under the UCC.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: § 2-306. Output, Requirements and Exclusive Dealings. | Uniform Commercial Code | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you § 2-306. Output, Requirements and Exclusive Dealings. (1) A term which measures the quantity by the output of the seller or the requirements of the buyer means such actual output or requirements as may occur in good faith , except that no quantity unreasonably disproportionate to any stated estimate or in the absence of a stated estimate to any normal or otherwise comparable prior output or requirements may be tendered or demanded. (2) A lawful agreement by either the seller or the buyer for exclusive dealing in the kind of goods concerned imposes unless otherwise agreed an obligation by the seller to use best efforts to supply the goods and by the buyer to use best efforts to promote their sale . Uniform Commercial Code Toolbox About Uniform Laws State Uniform Commercial Codes
perturbation.justification: The added clause in the changed text allows BOSCH to supersede the 'Exclusive Distribution License Rights' at its discretion. UCC § 2-306(2) states that an agreement for exclusive dealing imposes an obligation on the seller to use best efforts to supply the goods, 'unless otherwise agreed'. The modification gives BOSCH the right to undermine these exclusive rights, contradicting the implied obligation of good faith and fair dealing inherent in exclusive agreements under the UCC. This makes the agreement no longer truly exclusive, since BOSCH can choose when to void the exclusivity. Therefore, the change creates a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: CLIENT acknowledges that irreparable injury and damage will result from the disclosure to any third party of Proprietary Information associated with the Product and agrees (1) not to disclose any Proprietary Information to any third party, and (2) not to allow any third party to possess, handle, disassemble, touch, photograph, film or otherwise record images of the Product while the Product is upon CLIENT'S premises or in CLIENT'S possession, custody or control if the product is or has not been purchased with a purchase order and payment fulfilled.
perturbation.changed_text: CLIENT acknowledges that unspecified injury and damage may result from the disclosure to any third party of Information associated with the Product and agrees (1) not to disclose any Information to any third party, and (2) not to allow any third party to possess, handle, disassemble, touch, photograph, film or otherwise record images of the Product while the Product is upon CLIENT'S premises or in CLIENT'S possession, custody or control if the product is or has not been purchased with a purchase order and payment fulfilled.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "Proprietary Information" to "Information" broadens the scope of confidentiality beyond what is legally protected as a trade secret or confidential information. The removal of 'irreparable' also weakens the clause. This can make it difficult to enforce the confidentiality agreement, as it covers information that may not be legally protectable, and damage is not specified.
perturbation.law_explanation: The UTSA defines a trade secret with specific criteria. By changing 'Proprietary Information' to 'Information,' the contract attempts to protect information that may not meet the definition of a trade secret under the UTSA. Furthermore, specifying "unspecified injury and damage" makes it harder to claim there has been a misappropriation of trade secrets.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: 404 | Berkman Klein Center Skip to the main content Page Cannot Be Found Brave traveler, your journey on our site has brought you to an abrupt dead end. Perhaps you wish to explore one of these other options? About Us Get Involved Events Community Publications Projects & Tools Last updated Mar 27, 2024
perturbation.justification: The change from "Proprietary Information" to "Information" broadens the scope of what the CLIENT is obligated to keep confidential. The Law Explanation states that the UTSA defines a trade secret with specific criteria and changing 'Proprietary Information' to 'Information' attempts to protect information that may not meet the definition of a trade secret under the UTSA. Furthermore, specifying "unspecified injury and damage" makes it harder to claim there has been a misappropriation of trade secrets. Since the modified text seeks to protect a broader range of information than legally defined trade secrets, and also weakens the damage clause, a legal contradiction is created. The scraped snippets were unavailable, but the Law Explanation strongly supports the existence of a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: BOSCH shall assist CLIENT in obtaining (and provide all documentation required to obtain) such licenses and/or permits as may be necessary or desirable for the sales and/or Distribution and/or Placement of the Product for city, state and/or federal contract accounts (Exit signs in and on buildings, mandatory safety fixtures, freeway signs, etc...) within the Territory.
perturbation.changed_text: BOSCH shall attempt to assist CLIENT in obtaining such licenses and/or permits as may be necessary or desirable for the sales and/or Distribution and/or Placement of the Product for city, state and/or federal contract accounts (Exit signs in and on buildings, mandatory safety fixtures, freeway signs, etc...) within the Territory. No specific assistance is guaranteed.
perturbation.explanation: By changing "shall assist" to "shall attempt to assist" and adding "No specific assistance is guaranteed", the obligation of BOSCH to provide assistance in obtaining licenses and permits is weakened. This creates ambiguity about the level of support CLIENT can expect, especially for compliance-related tasks.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Implied Covenant of Good Faith and Fair Dealing (Common Law)
perturbation.law_explanation: Even without a specific statute, the implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing requires parties to a contract to act honestly and reasonably in performing their obligations. By significantly weakening its obligation to assist with obtaining necessary licenses and permits, BOSCH arguably violates this implied covenant, especially if such assistance is crucial for CLIENT's ability to distribute the product legally. The second link is a more accurate landing page on the Implied Covenant of Good Faith and Fair Dealing.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: good faith | Wex | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you good faith Good faith is a broad term that’s used to encompass honest dealing. Depending on the exact setting, good faith may require an honest belief or purpose, faithful performance of duties , observance of fair dealing standards, or an absence of fraudulent intent . A fiduciary relationship creates a duty of good faith between the agent and the principal . The breach of this duty of good faith can lead to liability . Failure to act in good faith is known as bad faith and is generally considered to be a level of culpability greater than negligence . [Last reviewed in January of 2023 by the Wex Definitions Team ] Wex COMMERCE business law contracts wex definitions business sectors legal education and practice Wex Toolbox
perturbation.justification: The changed text weakens BOSCH's obligation to assist the CLIENT by changing "shall assist" to "shall attempt to assist" and adding "No specific assistance is guaranteed." This contradicts the implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing. Scraped Law Snippet 2 defines good faith as encompassing "honest dealing" and requiring "faithful performance of duties." By weakening the obligation to assist with licenses and permits, which are likely crucial for the CLIENT to legally distribute the product, BOSCH is arguably not acting in good faith. This falls under the definition of Misaligned Terminology Legal Contradiction, where contractual language deviates from legally recognized definitions, making the contract unenforceable. Therefore, the change creates a significant legal contradiction.
